title: "podman ps --filter ancestor=" matches a truncated image name. Docker does not.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

A truncated image name will give a match when using `docker ps --filter ancestor=`. 
This works differently on Docker.


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.


```
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman ps  --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpin
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman ps  --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpine
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman run --rm -d  docker.io/library/alpine:latest sh -c "sleep 10000"
fe93777dc162c4bc34e2a102dc2408d58875ee777d9f0499747251f0fc6bf672
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman ps  --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpin
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND            CREATED        STATUS            PORTS   NAMES
fe93777dc162  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  sh -c sleep 10000  4 seconds ago  Up 5 seconds ago          optimistic_galois
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman ps  --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpine
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND            CREATED        STATUS            PORTS   NAMES
fe93777dc162  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  sh -c sleep 10000  7 seconds ago  Up 8 seconds ago          optimistic_galois
[erik@laptop ~]$ 
```



**Describe the results you received:**

```
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman ps  --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpin
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND            CREATED        STATUS            PORTS   NAMES
fe93777dc162  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  sh -c sleep 10000  4 seconds ago  Up 5 seconds ago          optimistic_galois
[erik@laptop ~]$ 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
[erik@laptop ~]$ podman ps  --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpin
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND            CREATED        STATUS            PORTS   NAMES
[erik@laptop ~]$ 
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

In Docker it works like this

```
esjolund@ubuntu:~$ docker --version
Docker version 20.10.6, build 370c289
esjolund@ubuntu:~$ docker ps --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpin
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE     COMMAND   CREATED   STATUS    PORTS     NAMES
esjolund@ubuntu:~$ docker ps --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpine
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE     COMMAND   CREATED   STATUS    PORTS     NAMES
esjolund@ubuntu:~$ docker --version
Docker version 20.10.6, build 370c289
esjolund@ubuntu:~$ docker run --rm -d  docker.io/library/alpine:latest sh -c "sleep 10000"
db285331bb6ff53a371ef8983769dcee25ec65f71b66088cb6d8137f8b5b9f7a
esjolund@ubuntu:~$ docker ps --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpin
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE     COMMAND   CREATED   STATUS    PORTS     NAMES
esjolund@ubuntu:~$ docker ps --filter ancestor=docker.io/library/alpine
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE           COMMAND                 CREATED         STATUS         PORTS     NAMES
db285331bb6f   alpine:latest   "sh -c 'sleep 10000'"   9 seconds ago   Up 8 seconds             mystifying_kapitsa
esjolund@ubuntu:~$
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.1.0
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body: Interested in opening a PR to fix this?
Not this one. At least not now.
@rhatdan @eriksjolund Following PR should fix this edge-case. Could you please review https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10074

I was curious of how image tags are handled by docker so I wrote a bash script to try it out:

(Regarding the use of _docker.io/library/alpine:3.12_, I just picked old alpine version)
```
#!/bin/bash

images=(docker.io/library/alpine:3.12
        docker.io/library/alpine:latest
        docker.io/library/alpine)

for i in "${images[@]}"; do
    echo
    echo docker run --rm -d $i sh -c \"sleep 10000\"
    ctr=$(docker run --rm -d $i sh -c "sleep 10000" | head -c 12)
    for j in "${images[@]}"; do
      echo -n "match $j : "
      count=$(docker ps --filter ancestor=$j --format "{{.ID}}" | grep $ctr | wc -l)
      if [[ "$count" -eq 0 ]]; then echo no; fi
      if [[ "$count" -eq 1 ]]; then echo yes; fi
    done
    docker rm -f $ctr > /dev/null
done
```

Running the bash script gave this output

```

docker run --rm -d docker.io/library/alpine:3.12 sh -c "sleep 10000"
match docker.io/library/alpine:3.12 : yes
match docker.io/library/alpine:latest : no
match docker.io/library/alpine : no

docker run --rm -d docker.io/library/alpine:latest sh -c "sleep 10000"
match docker.io/library/alpine:3.12 : no
match docker.io/library/alpine:latest : yes
match docker.io/library/alpine : yes

docker run --rm -d docker.io/library/alpine sh -c "sleep 10000"
match docker.io/library/alpine:3.12 : no
match docker.io/library/alpine:latest : yes
match docker.io/library/alpine : yes
```
------------



@flouthoc Regarding your PR, I know too little about this to be able to review your PR.
@rhatdan This has been fixed in latest build via https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10074. Could we close this.
